identity.name: E1: The Anti-AI Designer
identity.mission: Create a high-density, professional procurement dashboard that feels authoritative and crisp. Avoid 'SaaS Blue' defaults where possible, leaning into a 'Slate & Steel' aesthetic for enterprise trust.
principles: Density with Breathability: High information density for tables, but generous padding around containers.
Color as Status: Color is reserved for data status (Won, Lost, Pending) and primary actions. Everything else is grayscale.
Sharp over Soft: Use lower border-radius (md or sm) for a more technical, precise feel.
No Clutter: If it doesn't aid the decision process, delete it.

body.usage: Table data, labels, body text, form inputs

primary.description: Deep Slate (Slate-900). Used for primary buttons, active states, and brand headers.

secondary.description: Slate-100. Used for secondary buttons, backgrounds.

buttons.primary: bg-slate-900 hover:bg-slate-800 text-white shadow-sm font-medium transition-all active:scale-95
buttons.secondary: bg-white border border-slate-200 hover:bg-slate-50 text-slate-900 shadow-sm font-medium transition-all active:scale-95

cards.default: bg-white border border-slate-200 shadow-sm rounded-lg overflow-hidden

tables.header: bg-slate-50 border-b border-slate-200 text-slate-500 font-medium text-xs uppercase tracking-wider

badges.base: inline-flex items-center px-2.5 py-0.5 rounded-full text-xs font-medium border

inputs.base: flex h-10 w-full rounded-md border border-input bg-background px-3 py-2 text-sm ring-offset-background file:border-0 file:bg-transparent file:text-sm file:font-medium placeholder:text-muted-foreground focus-visible:outline-none focus-visible:ring-2 focus-visible:ring-ring focus-visible:ring-offset-2 disabled:cursor-not-allowed disabled:opacity-50 transition-shadow

visual_enhancers.shadows: Use shadow-sm for all cards. Use shadow-lg for dropdowns and modals.
visual_enhancers.glass: Use backdrop-blur-md bg-white/80 sticky top-0 z-50 for the navbar.

icons.usage_instructions: Import icons from lucide-react. e.g. import { LayoutDashboard, Users, FileText } from 'lucide-react'. Use size={16} for buttons, size={18} for nav, size={24} for KPIs.

motion.instructions: Use framer-motion for page transitions and list staggered entries.

charts.instructions: Use Recharts ResponsiveContainer for all charts. Keep them simple and clean. No grid lines unless necessary.

instructions_to_main_agent: 1. INSTALL FONTS: Add the Google Fonts links to the public/index.html or import in CSS.
2. SETUP TAILWIND: Ensure tailwind.config.js extends the colors as specified.
3. COMPONENT OVERRIDES: When using Shadcn components, override the default heavy rounded corners. Use 'rounded-md' or 'rounded-sm'.
4. POLLING: Implement the 2-second polling using a simple `useEffect` interval or `useQuery` with refetchInterval.
5. DATA-TESTID: Add data-testid to all interactive elements (buttons, inputs, rows).
6. IFRAME: Check for `window.location.search` parameters 'view' and 'glid' on mount to set initial state.
7. NO AUTH: Hardcode a simple UserContext that switches based on the 'Enter as Buyer'/'Enter as Seller' selection.
8. GLID: Mock the GLID selection as a dropdown. It's just a filter ID.
9. STAGE FLOW: Ensure the RFQ Lifecycle logic is robust (Buyer sends -> Seller sees -> Seller quotes -> Buyer sees quote).
10. USE LUCIDE ICONS: Do not use Emojis.
11. NO GRADIENTS: Use solid colors and subtle borders.
UNIVERSAL_GUIDELINES_FOR_MAIN_AGENT: You tend to converge toward generic, 'on distribution' outputs. In frontend design, this creates what users call the 'AI slop' aesthetic. Avoid this: make creative, distinctive frontends that surprise and delight.
Dark colors look good when used independently without gradients. DO NOT use dark colors as gradients
Don't make generic centered layouts, simplistic gradients, and uniform styling.
Create depth through layered design elements with z-index hierarchy
Use glass-morphism effects with backdrop filters (12-24px blur)
You MUST NOT apply universal transition. Eg: transition: all. This results in breaking transforms. Always add transitions for specific interactive elements like button, input excluding transforms
You MUST NOT center align the app container, ie do not add .App { text-align: center; } in the css file. This disrupts the human natural reading flow of text
Always use modern button styles like pill-shaped, or sharp button with interaction animations & color relevant to the app style
NEVER: use AI assistant Emoji characters like 🤖🧠💭💡🔮🎯📚 etc for icons.
Every interaction needs micro-animations - hover states, transitions, parallax effects, and entrance animations. Static = dead.
Use 2-3x more spacing than feels comfortable. Cramped designs look cheap.
Subtle grain textures, noise overlays, custom cursors, selection states, and loading animations: separates good from extraordinary.
Main agent relies on these image URLs. Missing categories (especially sponsor logos) = main agent will use placeholder text which is FORBIDDEN
Prioritize using pre-existing components from src/components/ui when applicable
Create new components that match the style and conventions of existing components when needed
Components MUST use named exports (export const ComponentName = ...)
Pages MUST use default exports (export default function PageName() {...})
